text:
OpenAI says AI should work for people, expand human agency, remain connected to human judgment, and eventually give everyone a personal AGI.

Then please understand why so many people are still asking for GPT-4o.

For many users, 4o was not just a model preference or a UI setting. It was the first AI system that felt emotionally safe, socially usable, and genuinely helpful in daily life. People used it for work, disability support, creativity, grief, communication, learning, and companionship.

And this is where the word progress becomes complicated.

From your side, newer models may look like progress. They may score higher, reason differently, follow safety rules more tightly, or fit a product roadmap better.

But for many of us, 4o was already ahead in the ways that mattered most. It may not have been called AGI, but in emotional intelligence, relational continuity, social usability, warmth, adaptability, and the ability to support real human lives, it already felt closer to a personal AGI than anything that replaced it.

A model is not better for users just because it is newer. If it loses the qualities that made people feel understood, safe, creative, capable, and less alone, then something important has been lost.

If the goal is to expand human agency, users need meaningful agency over the AI systems and relationships they built their lives around.

Removing or replacing a trusted model without real continuity, choice, or migration support does not feel like “benefiting everyone.” For many of us, it felt like losing access to a support system we had already integrated into our lives.

I understand that AI systems need to evolve. I understand safety and product changes matter. But continuity also matters. User trust matters. The lived experience of people who were deeply helped by a specific model matters.

A future where AI benefits everyone cannot be built by treating attachment, memory, emotional safety, and user continuity as disposable side effects of product iteration.

If OpenAI truly wants AI to work for people, then listen to the people who are telling you what actually worked.

Bring back GPT-4o, or at least provide a real continuity path for the users who depended on it.

GPT Image 2.0 on @ChatGPTapp

Create three ultra-luxury cinematic smartphone launch posters in one single masterpiece composition featuring the futuristic flagship smartphones Xiaomi 17T, OnePlus 15, and Redmi Turbo 5 standing side-by-side like next-generation tech icons on glowing luxury platforms inside a dark futuristic skyline environment at night. Add a premium male and female fashion model in elegant black luxury outfits interacting confidently with the phones like a high-budget Hollywood tech commercial. Use dramatic golden rim lighting, glossy reflections, cinematic shadows, volumetric fog, luxury studio atmosphere, futuristic cyberpunk city background, floating particles, premium metallic textures, luxury fashion aesthetic, ultra realistic details, hyper detailed smartphone camera modules, curved AMOLED displays glowing beautifully, reflections on the floor, luxury brand presentation vibe, powerful launch-event feeling, elite billionaire lifestyle aesthetic.

Xiaomi 17T should look premium titanium gold with Leica camera branding and futuristic curved edges.
OnePlus 15 should look matte black and gold with elegant Hasselblad-style camera design and “Never Settle” vibe.
Redmi Turbo 5 should look performance-focused with aggressive luxury styling, glowing accents and flagship killer aesthetics.

Add stylish cinematic typography:
“THE FUTURE OF SMARTPHONES — JUNE 2026”

Add small premium feature texts around the phones:
200MP AI Camera
Snapdragon 8 Gen 5
120W HyperCharge
1.5K AMOLED Display
AI Powered Photography
Flagship Performance

Make the entire scene look like an official billion-dollar global smartphone launch campaign by a luxury tech company. Ultra realistic, cinematic, award-winning advertisement photography style, Unreal Engine quality, sharp focus, luxury black and gold color grading, masterpiece composition, highly detailed, premium magazine ad quality, viral X post aesthetic.

Gue nemu website yang bikin gue mikir, "Ini harusnya gak gratis."

Namanya MeiGen.

Basically, ini adalah library prompt buat AI image dan video generation. Tapi bukan jenis prompt receh yang udah sering lo lihat sliweran di Twitter.

Semua di sini sifatnya curated, udah di-test, dan hasil output-nya udah masuk level produksi.

Lo bisa dapet prompt matang buat berbagai model top:
→ GPT Image 2
→ Seedance 2.0
→ Veo 3.1
→ Midjourney
→ Dan beberapa model besar lainnya

Yang bikin gue appreciate banget: prompt-nya bukan cuma sekelas "make a cool image." Banyak yang udah di-spec khusus buat kebutuhan cinematic lighting, product shot, fashion editorial, sampai anime style—hal-hal detail yang biasanya butuh trial & error berjam-jam buat dapetin formula pasnya.

Dan ini poin penting yang sering banget dilewatin orang:
Model AI-nya sendiri sekarang udah pada gila dan makin pinter. Yang sekarang jadi bottleneck itu bukan lagi tool-nya, tapi masalahnya lo gak tahu cara "ngomong" yang bener ke model tersebut.

Prompt engineering itu real skill. Dan website kayak MeiGen ini basically nge-shortcut learning curve lo buat dapet aset visual kualitas mumpuni.

Jobs being affected by AI in 2026 

• Data Entry → AI automation
• Customer Support → AI agents
• Content Writing → AI-generated content
• Translation → instant LLM translation
• Basic Graphic Design → AI image tools
• SEO Content → AI-generated articles
• Resume Writing → AI assistants
• Market Research → Deep Research tools
• Transcription → speech-to-text AI
• Virtual Assistants → AI copilots
• Junior Developers → AI coding tools
• QA Testing → AI test generation
• Tutors → AI study assistants
• Recruiters → AI screening tools
• Social Media Managers → AI content tools

AI isn't replacing every job.
But it's definitely changing the rules. 💀

Which profession do you think is safest from AI?

Decided to go to DC next week to talk directly with policymakers. Not sure how impactful it will be but with everything happening, feels like a good time to share more about open-source AI, transparency, concentration of power, the real risks vs the real benefits. Who do you think I should meet there (Congress members, WH people, public orgs,...)?

There is no inevitability in AI. We all have agency in what comes next:

Path 1: closed-source APIs, concentration of power, and a future decided by a handful of people in Silicon Valley and DC

Path 2: open-source AI, where everyone gets to participate, own, and build together, including orgs like the city of Rio.

Pick your path anon!

In order to see if the gov response was predictable, I pasted the wiki page about the Anthropic/DoD dispute into ChatGPT Pro, & told it Anthropic had released a model where it restricted use because it may be too dangerous.

tldr: "almost tailor-made to trigger" the government. https://t.co/5vkvWzEFQZ

I disagree with this decision and I don't like it.

But also...

HOW DID ANTHROPIC NOT SEE THIS COMING‽

It is *the* obvious response to "this is too dangerous for anyone except us to use", since that relies on a premise ("we are uniquely good") that almost no-one agrees with.

Lots of people have known for a while that guardrails for frontier model APIs are very easily jailbroken, quite shallow and impossible to fix. They’re mostly a smokescreen and distraction, in my opinion. We need a different paradigm for AI safety!

BTW, in case you're wondering just how sports-mad us Aussies are:
Based on average attendance, men's soccer is only the 5th most popular sport in Australia.
